briefdescription.para: Allocates memory for co_nmt_hb_t object using allocator from can_net_t.
Frees memory allocated for co_nmt_hb_t object.
The CAN receive callback function for a heartbeat consumer.
The CAN timer callback function for a heartbeat consumer.
Returns the alignment (in bytes) of the co_nmt_hb_t structure.
Returns the size (in bytes) of the co_nmt_hb_t structure.
Creates a new CANopen NMT heartbeat consumer service.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CAN network.
a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.

simplesect.para: a pointer to a new heartbeat consumer service, or NULL on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Destroys a CANopen NMT heartbeat consumer service.
Processes the value of CANopen object 1016 (Consumer heartbeat time) for the specified heartbeat consumer.

detaileddescription.para: If the node-ID is valid and the heartbeat time is non-zero, the heartbeat consumer is activated. Note that this only activates the CAN frame receiver for heartbeat messages. The CAN timer for heartbeat events is not activated until the first heartbeat message is received or co_nmt_hb_set_st() is invoked.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a heartbeat consumer service.

briefdescription.para: Sets the expected state of a remote NMT node.

detaileddescription.para: If the heartbeat consumer is active, invocation of this function is equivalent to reception of a heartbeat message with the specified state and will (re)activate the CAN timer for heartbeat events.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a heartbeat consumer service.
the state of the node (excluding the toggle bit).

briefdescription.para: This file is part of the CANopen library; it contains the implementation of the NMT heartbeat consumer functions.

detaileddescription.para:
